reflection:
the speech is done. on the 1st and 2nd you were running anxiety at 6, stress at 7, heart pounding, repeating lines in your sleep, freaking out a few hours before. then yesterday anxiety dropped to 2 and stress to 2. you walked into the thing you were scared of and you did it. that drop is real. don't skip past it on your way to the next worry.

but look what showed up the second the pressure lifted. throat worse, nostrils closing, mucus, feverish, head aching. that feverish feeling was already there on the 2nd when you were terrified. this is your body presenting the bill now that the threat passed. it held together for the speech, then let go. getting sick right after a big stressor is one of the most normal things a nervous system does.

here's the part you already know, because you wrote it. "i'm avoiding doing what i'm supposed to, which is rest." and in the same breath you reached for weight training to bring cortisol down. i get the logic, but not today. a body fighting something off doesn't need strain, it needs you horizontal. the trip just started, you've got days.

one thing for today: kill the training instinct, and the ping pong too if your head is bad. let yourself actually rest, the kind where you do nothing and don't feel guilty. that's the work today.

proud of you for the speech. 🙏
opener: speech is done, anxiety crashed 6 to 2. now your body wants rest.
focus_for_today: skip training and ping pong today. you're getting sick, the real win is actual rest.
